author: Dr. Marcus Hale / Dr. Priya Nair (Transplant Hepatology)
content:
POST-TRANSPLANT HEPATOLOGY CLINIC — UNIVERSITY
Date: 9/15/2027 (~2 months post-OLT)

INTERVAL: Doing well. No dyspnea, no abdominal distension. Energy improving; back to light activity. Adherent with immunosuppression. Remains sober and engaged in recovery.

EXAM/DATA:
- Euvolemic; abdomen soft, non-distended, no fluid wave; lungs clear bilaterally.
- Weight down to ~63 kg (dry), well below her decompensated weight (~75-77 kg with ascites).
- Labs (LAB003): bilirubin 0.9, INR 1.1, albumin 3.8, normal transaminases; Na 139, Cr 1.0; platelets recovering (118 K); tacrolimus trough therapeutic.
- Post-transplant Doppler US (IMG005): patent vasculature, NO ascites.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
- Excellent early graft function. The ascites and right hepatic hydrothorax have RESOLVED; she no longer requires paracentesis or thoracentesis, and the PleurX has been providing negligible output and is planned for removal.
- Diuretics (spironolactone/furosemide), lactulose, and rifaximin DISCONTINUED.
- Continue immunosuppression and post-transplant surveillance; continue recovery program.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Marcus Hale, MD and Dr. Priya Nair, MD (Transplant Hepatology). 9/15/2027.
